title: Thermal Management in Combat
summary: Managing heat buildup during combat operations.
details: In space, heat only dissipates through radiation - no convection or conduction to atmosphere. Weapons, engines, and reactors generate heat. Heat buildup causes: weapon malfunction, crew discomfort, component failure. Radiators must have line of sight to cold space. Radiators are vulnerable - damage reduces cooling capacity. Extended combat at high power causes thermal runaway. Manage heat as a critical resource.
annotations: Thermal limits often determine engagement duration.
Facing radiators away from sun improves cooling.
